data.title: NIO Looks Ready to Rise. What It Means for Chinese EV Stocks. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Doug Busch

After months of lagging behind the broader market, Chinese electric-vehicle stocks may be starting to stir again.

Improving technical setups and renewed interest in beaten-down growth names are bringing the group back onto investors' radar, with NIO beginning to show signs of stabilization. The stock's recent price action, notably following a powerful earnings advance Tuesday, suggests selling pressure may be fading, raising the possibility that the EV maker could be preparing for a rebound if sentiment toward China's auto sector continues to improve.

It is also good to see peers in the region acting well too. One never wants to see a single stock shouldering all the weight of a group. XPeng is very likely to end its three-week losing streak this week; it was up 14% as of Thursday afternoon. Of course Tesla is the giant in the room. It is teetering on its 200 day simple moving average and trading between the very round $400-$500 numbers in what looks like a bearish head and shoulders pattern.

Our focus today is on NIO and let's take a detailed look at its daily and weekly charts to support our bullish narrative.

Looking at its daily chart shows some positive traits. First notice the bullish RSI divergence with the higher RSI lows as price made a new low. This is a good indication that downward momentum in the stock is coming to an end. Big volume spikes are backing up price advances, always a sign of institutional interest.

The stock has recaptured both its 50 and 200 day simple moving averages, both of which are now sloping higher. Admire how the stock successfully retested a bull flag breakout from last August. A double bottom base has now set up with a pivot of $5.79. That trigger came from a bearish island reversal completed with the gap down of 7.3% on Dec. 31. The recent lows were buoyed by a bullish hammer candle on March 3 and the very next session finished a bullish island reversal. Look for the stock to move toward $8 by the second half, which would be a gain of 42% from current prices. Remain bullish above $4.75.

NIO ADR was trading around $5.60 Thursday.

Looking at its five year weekly chart shows a nice bottoming process under way. Since the start of 2024 the stock has been consolidating after a long downtrend that began in the upper $60s in early 2021 and ended only when the stock traded in single digits just two years later. The right clavicle in a bullish inverse head and shoulders formation has started to take place since last October and I was encouraged by last week's doji candle and this week's reclaim of the 50-week simple moving average, with the stock now 19% higher. If that sticks by tomorrow's close, it would be the best weekly advance since late last August. Notice how accumulation has been on full display since last summer, as the stock has refused to go lower. On this longer time frame, if the $8 inverse head and shoulders breakout is achieved later in 2026, look for a move toward $13 by early 2027.

Should the current setup hold, NIO could be gearing up for its next move higher.

Doug Busch is the senior technical analyst at Barron's Investor Circle . His technical view is added to stock picks, including those published exclusively for Investor Circle readers. A glossary of technical terms is updated regularly with new entries.

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: VanE 10-K: NAV $1.38B, NAV per Share $24.73, Net Income swing from $419M to -$194M
data.description:
VanE reported a NAV of $1,382.27 million at December 31, 2025, up from $1,280.45 million a year earlier, while NAV per share fell to $24.73 from $26.40 as bitcoin price movements and portfolio valuations shifted. The trust recorded net income of $418.97 million in 2024 driven by unrealized appreciation and realized gains, and a net decrease from operations of $194.00 million in 2025 reflecting unrealized depreciation despite higher realized gains. Investment results and share creation/redemption activity were central drivers of year-over-year changes in NAV and per-share values.

Financial Highlights

  • NAV (Total Assets): $1,280.45 million at 12/31/2024; $1,382.27 million at 12/31/2025 (7.95% YoY increase).
  • Net Income: $418.97 million (2024 net increase from operations); $(194.00) million (2025 net decrease from operations).
  • Net Income Per Share / NAV per Share: NAV per Share $26.40 at 12/31/2024; $24.73 at 12/31/2025 (reflects bitcoin price moves).
  • Investment Realized/Unrealized Results: 2024 — unrealized appreciation $384.61 million; realized gain $34.41 million. 2025 — unrealized depreciation $278.76 million; realized gain $84.76 million.

Business Highlights

  • NAV Growth & Scale: Trust NAV expanded substantially from roughly $100,000 in 2023 to $1.38 billion by end of 2025, driven by large share creations and movements in the underlying bitcoin price.
  • Shares Issuance Activity: Significant primary market activity with net creation of millions of shares via Authorized Participants, supporting inflows and enabling portfolio growth through basket creations and redemptions.
  • Product Objective & Operations: Operates as a passive bitcoin-tracking trust that issues and redeems only in 25,000-share baskets and holds bitcoin with a custodian to track the reference asset.
  • Price Sensitivity: NAV per share closely follows bitcoin price volatility — substantial appreciation in 2024 (approx. 111% increase in referenced period) followed by about a 6% decline in 2025, driving corresponding NAV per share movements.
  • Expense & Liquidity Management: Sponsor absorbed most expenses through fee waivers; liquidity management depends on bitcoin sales and authorized participant creations/redemptions to meet cash needs and maintain operations.

Original SEC Filing: VanEck Bitcoin ETF [ HODL ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Crypto accounting startup Cryptio lands $45M as institutions move onchain
data.description:
Cryptio, an accounting and data platform focused on regulated digital assets, has raised $45 million in a Series B funding round, highlighting growing demand for tools that help financial institutions reconcile and report blockchain-based transactions within traditional accounting systems.

The round was co-led by venture firms BlackFin Capital Partners and Sentinel Global, with participation from 1kx, BlueYard Capital, Alven and Ledger Cathay Capital. 

Cryptio develops software that helps companies reconcile activity across wallets, custodians and exchanges, translating blockchain transaction data into accounting records used for financial reporting, audits and compliance.

The company says it serves more than 400 enterprise clients and has processed over $3 trillion in transaction volume. Its clients include crypto companies such as Circle, Gemini and Securitize, as well as traditional financial institutions, including Société Générale’s SG-Forge.

Several other companies operate in the same niche as Cryptio, highlighting the emergence of a small but growing market for crypto accounting and financial reporting infrastructure. Companies such as Lukka, TaxBit, Bitwave and CoinLedger offer software that helps businesses reconcile blockchain transactions and convert them into records used for tax reporting, audits and regulatory compliance.

Demand for tokenized finance infrastructure continues to grow

Cryptio’s growth is also being fueled by rising institutional interest in tokenized assets, which require accounting systems capable of recording and reconciling blockchain-based financial activity.

Sidra Pervez, senior vice president at tokenization firm Securitize, said maintaining accurate financial records across capital markets is becoming more important as traditional finance expands into tokenized securities.

Loic Fonteneau, managing director at BlackFin Capital Partners, said “digital assets are becoming embedded within regulated financial markets,” which requires “institutional-grade infrastructure” to support accounting, tokenized asset reporting and lending.

Major financial institutions are increasingly participating in tokenization, with the likes of HSBC, BNP Paribas and Goldman Sachs backing the tokenization-focused Canton Foundation. The industry group supports the development and governance of the Canton Network, a blockchain designed for regulated financial markets.

In January, State Street announced the rollout of a new crypto tokenization tool to help clients create tokenized money market funds, exchange-traded funds and tokenized deposits.

While estimates vary, industry data shows that the total value of tokenized real-world assets, excluding stablecoins, has surpassed $26 billion, with much of the demand coming from private credit and US Treasurys-backed funds. 

Other fast-growing segments include tokenized money market funds — blockchain-based versions of traditional funds that invest in short-term government debt and other low-risk securities.

data.title: CBOT wheat ends higher as crude oil rally stokes commodities buying
data.description:
Chicago Board of Trade wheat futures rose for a second straight session on Thursday on fund and speculative buying tied to surging crude oil prices as the war in the Middle East continued to escalate, traders said.

  • Wheat futures also firmed on signs of improved demand. Wheat is often sensitive to geopolitical risks and also benefits from an influx of managed money into commodity investments.

  • Oil prices rose about 8% on Thursday as Iran stepped up attacks on oil and transport facilities across the Middle East, and the country's supreme leader said the closure of the vital Strait of Hormuz should continue.

  • The U.S. Department of Agriculture reported net U.S. wheat export sales in the week ended March 5 at 495,789 metric tons, at the high end of a range of analyst estimates and the largest in a month.

  • CBOT May soft red winter wheat (WK26) ended up 3-3/4 cents at $5.94-3/4 per bushel.

  • K.C. May hard red winter wheat (KWK26) last traded a penny lower at $6.12-1/2 per bushel.

  • Minneapolis May spring wheat (MWEK26) last traded 3 cents lower at $6.35 per bushel.

data.title: Eli Lilly Stock Slips After Warning on Compounded GLP-1 Drugs With Additives
data.description:
Shares of Eli Lilly and Company slipped about 1% in early Thursday trading after the drugmaker cautioned about potential safety concerns tied to compounded versions of tirzepatide mixed with vitamin B12.

The company said testing of certain compounded products detected notable levels of an impurity that may form when vitamin B12 reacts chemically with tirzepatide, the active ingredient in Lilly's weight-loss treatment Zepbound. The presence of such impurities could raise potential safety concerns, including possible toxicity or immune-related reactions.

Lilly also indicated that some compounders appear to be combining tirzepatide with other additives, including glycine, pyridoxine, niacinamide, and carnitine. According to the company, these substances do not have established clinical benefits when paired with tirzepatide and could introduce additional risks for patients.

Compounded medications, customized drugs prepared by pharmacies, were initially allowed in the United States to help address shortages of popular weight-loss treatments during a surge in demand. However, the U.S. Food and Drug Administration has since determined that the shortage of these medicines has been resolved, limiting the circumstances under which such compounded versions can be offered.

The agency has also indicated it may tighten oversight of GLP-1 active pharmaceutical ingredients used in non-approved compounded drugs marketed as alternatives to branded treatments. Companies such as Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) have promoted similar offerings through telehealth platforms.

data.title: Crude Oil Skyrockets As Iran Toughens Its Position Against U.S., Israel
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Crude oil catapulted on Thursday as new leadership in Iran declines to bow down to U.S. pressure in the raging Middle East war and instead vowed revenge, threatened U.S. allies, and resolved to ramp up attacks.

WTI Crude Oil for April delivery was last seen trading up by $7.45 (or 8.54%) at $94.70 per barrel.

The Middle East war that began following the U.S.-Israeli combined attacks on Iran on February 28 entered day number 13 today without any sign of a de-escalation.

In his maiden message to the public after assuming office, Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei (son of slain leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei) issued a stern warning to Iran's neighbors against harboring U.S. bases.

Khamenei's address was read by a news anchor on state television in which he vowed to avenge all Iranians killed in the war.

Expressing gratitude to the 'Resistance Front' comprising Yemen's Houthi militia and Lebanon's Hezbollah, Khamenei also affirmed that Iran will never retreat in this war but would resist with 'full force.'

Asserting Iran would obtain compensation from its 'enemy,' Khamenei went on to add that the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz will continue as Iran wants to use it as a tool to pressure the U.S. and Israel to halt their attacks.

The 55-kilometer-wide waterway between Iran and Oman which separates the Persian Gulf from the Arabian sea is critical for the transit of around one-fifth of global oil shipments with nearly 13 million barrels of oil per day moving through these waters.

Already, Iran has avowed not to let even a liter of oil to pass through the channel.

Yesterday, Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian suggested that, for the war to end, Iran should be compensated for war-inflicted damages and be offered international assurances against future attacks.

After reports of Iran laying water mines near the Strait of Hormuz surfaced, U.S. President Donald Trump warned the nation of unprecedented consequences if it does not remove those mines immediately though Iran brushed aside the threats.

Late-night attacks on two fuel tankers (Safesea Vishnu and Zefyros) in Iraqi waters by explosive-laden Iranian boats set them ablaze, prompting Iraq to shut down its oil ports.

Oman has ordered an evacuation of vessels from the country's Mina Al Fahal oil port after Wednesday's Iranian attack at the Port of Salalah.

The International Energy Administration on Wednesday announced the release of 400 million barrels from the strategic reserves of member countries. Despite the planned intervention, markets remain focused on the large-scale disruption due to the deepening conflict in the Middle East.

Transit obstacles through the Strait of Horzmuz has resulted in storage constraints for several Arab nations, forcing them to scale down their output production.

Ebrahim Zolfaqari, the spokesperson for Iran's Khatam al-Anbiya military command headquarters stated that the world must 'get ready for oil to be $200 a barrel.'

Meanwhile, despite the severe impact on global supply chains caused by the U.S.-Israel versus Iran war, in its latest monthly report, the Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries maintained its 2026-27 global oil demand growth projection, with the forecast for 2026 at 1.38 million barrels per day and for 2027 at 1.3 million barrels per day.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: Aptera Motors Stock Falls on Warrant Inducement to Raise $6.3M
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Shares of Aptera Motors tumbled after the company announced a warrant inducement to raise gross cash proceeds of about $6.3 million.

The stock fell 20% to $2.74 a share in midday Thursday trading. Shares are down 66% over the past 12 months.

Aptera, which is developing a solar-powered electric vehicle, said that investors had exercised warrants to purchase about 3.17 million shares for roughly $6.3 million worth of stock.

The company agreed to issue five-year warrants to purchase up to about 4.75 million shares at an exercise price of $3.50 a share in consideration for the cash exercise of the existing warrants. The transaction is expected to close around Friday.

Aptera said the combination of recent warrant exercises has raised about $8.1 million in gross cash proceeds. It plans to use the proceeds from the transaction to continue developing its vehicles and for other working capital and general corporate purposes.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Hims & Hers Stock Jumps After Novo Nordisk Deal. What It Means for Drugmaker
data.description:
Shares of Hims & Hers Health climbed about 5% on Thursday morning after the telehealth company said it entered a collaboration with Novo Nordisk to offer the drugmaker's branded GLP-1 weight-loss treatments on its platform.

Under the arrangement, Hims & Hers plans to provide Novo Nordisk's diabetes and obesity medications, including Ozempic (semaglutide) and Wegovy (semaglutide), beginning later in March. The drugs are widely used GLP-1 therapies that help regulate blood sugar and appetite, supporting treatment for diabetes and chronic weight management.

As part of the collaboration, Hims & Hers said it will discontinue marketing its compounded GLP-1 products across its platform and promotional channels. Compounded medications are customized formulations prepared by pharmacies when commercially available drugs may not meet specific patient needs.

The company noted that compounded GLP-1 options could still be prescribed in limited situations if a healthcare provider determines that a compounded version is clinically necessary for a patient. However, such products will no longer be actively promoted by the telehealth provider.

The agreement appears aimed at expanding access to branded GLP-1 therapies while aligning the company more closely with established pharmaceutical suppliers as demand for weight-loss treatments continues to grow.

data.title: Rapid Micro Biosystems Falls on Wider-Than-Expected 4Q Loss
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Shares of Rapid Micro Biosystems fell after the company posted a wider-than-expected loss in the fourth quarter.

Shares were down 20% to $3.49 in Thursday afternoon trading. The stock has gained 46% in the past year.

The life sciences technology company on Thursday reported a loss of $12.5 million, or 28 cents a share, compared with a loss of $9.67 million, or 22 cents a share, a year earlier.

Fourth-quarter revenue rose to $11.3 million, up from $8.22 million a year prior.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet were expecting a loss of 23 cents a share and revenue of $11.4 million.

The company also said Thursday that it had received a follow-on multi-system order for its Growth Direct platform from Samsung Biologics.

For the current year, Rapid Micro is expecting revenue of $37 million to $41 million, with between 30 to 38 Growth Direct system placements.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Bridge Data Centres Plans S$5 Billion AI Investment in Singapore
data.description:
Bridge Data Centres is preparing a major capital push into artificial intelligence infrastructure, as demand for high-performance computing continues to reshape Asia's data-center landscape. The Bain Capital-backed company plans to invest as much as S$5 billion ($3.9 billion) in AI technology development in Singapore, according to chief executive Eric Fan. The initiative is expected to focus on research programs and facilities tied to advanced power architectures, next-generation cooling systems, AI-enabled operations, and energy optimization designed for high-density computing environments.

Fan said Singapore will serve as the primary base for the effort, with the company also planning to recruit technical and research talent to support AI-focused facilities and innovation initiatives. Bridge Data Centres is simultaneously working with several partners to develop AI infrastructure technologies as it expands across Asia. The company is targeting as much as 3 gigawatts of global capacity by 2030, positioning itself among data-center operators increasing their presence across markets including India and Southeast Asia as AI developers drive new demand for computing infrastructure.

One project under development involves Singapore's first floating hydrogen power generation solution, which Bridge Data Centres is building in partnership with Concord New Energy (CWPWF). The barge-based platform is intended to supply electricity for future AI data centers. The effort comes as the global race to build AI infrastructure accelerates, with technology companies and investors directing large sums into facilities equipped with advanced chips and cooling systems, even as some observers question whether the scale of investment will ultimately generate economic returns that justify the spending.

data.title: Precious Metals Fall as Oil Jumps — Market Talk
data.description: Gold and silver futures settle lower asWTI crude oil surgesto $95 a barrel. The upticks in crude oil along with a stronger U.S. dollar have been common in the trading following the onset of the war with Iran, this while investors back out of riskier assets. If the war drags into next month, then crude oil prices may have more upside, says David Mericle of Goldman Sachs in a note. Mericle says that Goldman Sachs pegs Brent crude at an average of $110 a barrel if the war is active going into April. Front-month gold settles down 1% to $5,115.80 per troy ounce, and silver drops 0.5% to $84.67 an ounce. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: CBOT corn firms as crude oil prices surge on Mideast war
data.description:
Chicago Board of Trade corn futures firmed for a second straight session on Thursday as fund and speculative buying tied to surging crude oil prices more than offset pressure from ample global grain supplies, traders said.

  • Oil prices rose about 8% on Thursday as Iran stepped up attacks on oil and transport facilities across the Middle East, and the country's supreme leader said the closure of the vital Strait of Hormuz should continue.

  • Corn futures were also supported by expectations that U.S. farmers will plant fewer corn acres this spring as fertilizer and fuel prices are spiking in the wake of the closing of the Strait of Hormuz.

  • The U.S. Department of Agriculture reported net U.S. corn export sales in the week ended on March 5 at 1,503,776 metric tons, down 31% from a week earlier but in line with analyst estimates. Season-to-date sales remain nearly 32% above last year.

  • CBOT May corn (CK26) closed 2-1/4 cents higher at $4.62-1/2 per bushel.

  • July 2027 through July 2028 contracts posted new life-of-contract highs during the session.

data.title: Vivendi Swings To Profit In FY25
data.description:
PARIS (dpa-AFX) - Vivendi SE (VIVEF.PK) Thursday reported earnings attributed to shareholders of 20 million euros or 0.02 euros per share for the full year 2025, compared to a loss of 6,004 million euros or 5.96 euros per share for the full year of 2024.

EBITA was positive at 45 million euros, compared to a loss of 1 million euros in 2024, reflecting the performance of Gameloft and the reduction of the headquarters' operating costs.

In 2025, Vivendi's revenues were 307 million euros, compared to 297 million euros in 2024, an increase of 3.5%. At constant currency and perimeter, Vivendi's revenues increased by 4.3% compared to 2024. This increase reflects the good performance of Gameloft, which contributed €303 million to revenues.

Yannick Bolloré, Chairman of Vivendi's Supervisory Board, and Arnaud de Puyfontaine, Chief Executive Officer, said: '2025 was a year of transition for Vivendi following the split of the Group in mid-December 2024. The efforts undertaken over several years to reposition Gameloft in a challenging video games sector proved particularly successful. We also sold our telecoms holdings in Italy and Spain to refocus on a portfolio of activities in content, media, and entertainment.'

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

data.title: CBOT soybeans rally to 21-month peak on surging crude oil
data.description:
Chicago Board of Trade soybean futures rallied to the highest level since May 2024 as crude oil extended gains as investors wrestled with disruptions caused by the U.S.-Israel war on Iran.

  • Oil prices rose about 8% on Thursday as Iran stepped up attacks on oil and transport facilities across the Middle East, and the country's supreme leader said the closure of the vital Strait of Hormuz should continue.

  • Higher crude prices can support soybeans as soyoil is widely used to produce biodiesel fuel. Soy processing margins have also swelled as the price of soyoil, a key biodiesel feedstock, has firmed.

  • Expectations that the U.S. government may soon release final biofuel blending rules were also supportive. Proposed rules suggest demand for soyoil to make crop-based diesel fuel would rise.

  • Investors are adjusting positions in the hope that there is a breakthrough in upcoming trade talks between the United States and top soy importer China.

  • The U.S. Department of Agriculture reported net U.S. soybean export sales in the week ended on March 5 at 466,258 metric tons, in line with analyst estimates and the largest in three weeks.

  • CBOT May soybeans (SK26) settled 13-1/4 cents higher at $12.28-1/4 per bushel after notching a contract high of $12.38-3/4. It was the highest level for a most-active contract since May 28, 2024.

  • Most soybean contracts posted new life-of-contract highs during the session.

  • CBOT May soyoil (BOK26) ended up 0.26 cent to finish at 67.42 cents per pound.

  • CBOT May soymeal (SMK26) rose $4.80 to end at $320.20 per short ton.

data.title: Microsoft Exec VP of Experiences and Devices Jha to Retire
data.description:
By Josh Beckerman

Rajesh Jha, Microsoft's executive vice president of experiences and devices, plans to retire on July 1.

Jha's roles at the company have included corporate vice president for Office 365 and executive vice president of the Office Products Group.

"Our priorities around SFI, QEI, and Copilot remain unchanged--let's keep the intensity here," Jha said in a post on Microsoft's blog.

In the post, Jha said Jeff Teper, the president for collaborative apps and platforms, would be promoted to executive vice president of the group. Corporate Vice President for the Office Product Group Sumit Chauhan and Kirk Koenigsbauer, corporate vice president of the experiences and devices group, also would be promoted to presidents of the group.

In February, Phil Spencer, Microsoft's head of gaming, retired.

Write to Josh Beckerman at josh.beckerman@wsj.com

data.title: Cocoa prices sink after recent gains, coffee rises
data.description:
Cocoa prices fell sharply on Thursday, as investors took the view that the past eight days of successive gains were overdone given the market surplus, while coffee prices rallied.

COCOA

* London cocoa ​settled down 79 pounds, or 3.2%, to 2,404 pounds per metric ton, after hitting a three-week high of 2,510 pounds on Wednesday.

* Dealers said cocoa is running out of steam, having pushed higher after Ivory Coast's move to slash government-set farmer prices triggered a pick up in demand for mid-crop purchases by traders.

* In other news, cocoa farmers in Brazil have slammed the brakes on new planting projects following a 70% plunge in prices from their 2024 peaks, stalling growth that investors had expected would make the country a major bean supplier.

* New York cocoa lost 3.3% to $3,315 a ton.

COFFEE

* Arabica coffee settled up 4.5 cents, or 1.6%, at $2.919 per lb, having settled down 2.8% on Wednesday.

* Coffee is under pressure from the prospect of a bumper crop in Brazil this year but against that it is getting some support from the U.S.-Israel war on Iran, which has severely disrupted global shipping flows and boosted energy prices, dealers said.

* Dealers noted that the price premiums over futures, also known as differentials, continue to rise at producing countries such as Brazil and Colombia, as farmers that are well capitalized after some years of good prices resist selling at current smaller market prices.

* Robusta coffee rose 2% to $3,625 a ton.

SUGAR

* Raw sugar settled up 0.13 cent, or 0.9%, at 14.38 cents per lb.

* The market has derived support recently from gains in energy prices linked to the Iran war, but has been unable to break out of its 13-15 cents range.

* Brazil's state-controlled oil company, Petrobras, which supplies around 80% of the gasoline in Brazil, has yet to increase local prices but will be under increasing pressure to do so if the conflict persists.

* Higher gasoline prices in Brazil should make it more profitable for sugar mills to use cane to produce biofuel ethanol rather than sugar.

* "The longer that oil prices remain elevated, the more focus the sugar market will place on Petrobras and its potential pricing decisions," Rabobank said in a note.

* White sugar gained 0.2% to $414.30 a ton.

data.title: McEwen Reports Q4 Net Income $38.1M, Full-Year Net Income $34.4M; Revenue $197.6M
data.description:
McEwen reported a fourth-quarter 2025 net income of $38.1 million ($0.70 per share) versus a Q4 2024 net loss of $8.2 million, and full-year 2025 net income of $34.4 million ($0.64 per share). Revenue for the year rose to $197.6 million driven by higher realized gold prices and sales of 58,552 gold-equivalent ounces (GEOs). Adjusted EBITDA for full-year 2025 was $66.2 million ($1.22 per share).

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $197.6 million for the year ended December 31, 2025 (Q4 2025 revenue $64.6 million).
  • Gross profit: $47.6 million for full-year 2025 (Q4 2025 gross profit $17.4 million).
  • Operating income: $2.7 million for full-year 2025 (operating income for 2024 was a loss of $50.6 million).
  • Net income: $34.4 million for full-year 2025; Q4 2025 net income $38.1 million ($0.70 per share) vs. Q4 2024 net loss $8.2 million ($0.16 per share).
  • Adjusted EBITDA: $66.2 million for full-year 2025 ($1.22 per share); Q4 2025 adjusted EBITDA $28.1 million ($0.51 per share).

Business Highlights

  • Production and growth targets: Company plans to increase production to 250,000–300,000 GEOs by 2030 through expansion across Canada, USA, Mexico and Argentina.
  • Los Azules (copper) progress: Feasibility study completed (published Oct 7, 2025); RIGI approval received (30-year incentives and regulatory stability); aiming for FID by end of 2026 and construction targeted for early 2027.
  • Asset development: Stock Mine (Fox Complex) on schedule and on budget for initial production mid-2026 and commercial production in 2027; Grey Fox PFS targeted for Q2 2026 after a resource update increasing indicated ounces by 23%.
  • Gold Bar Mine Complex: Updated Lookout Mountain M&I resource of 402,300 oz and ongoing plans to integrate Lookout Mountain, Windfall and Trinity Ridge to target 90,000–110,000 GEOs annually from the complex.
  • San José and El Gallo: San José (49% attributable) delivered strong Q4 production and reduced unit costs, with dividends expected to fund growth; El Gallo Phase 1 construction to start mid-2026 with targeted mid-2027 production (~20,000 GEOs/year).

Original SEC Filing: McEwen Inc. [ MUX ] - 8-K - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Descartes Systems CEO Says AI Will Boost Value of Full-Service Tech Providers — Market Talk
data.description: Descartes Systems CEO Edward Ryan says markets have it wrong about how artificial intelligence will affect business. Ryan says on an analyst call that he has heard commentators raise concerns that "AI could kill existing technology companies." However, Ryan says "those commentators are fundamentally misunderstanding the value technology companies bring to customers." Instead, Ryan says AI will increase the value of companies like Descartes because customers rely on far more than software code. What they're really buying is "a service that includes security, trust, stability, compliance, infrastructure, operational and customer support, workflow and domain expertise, proprietary data, connections, scale, innovation, cross-pollination of valuable ideas and, yes, technology functionality that is powered by software." (adriano.marchese@wsj.com)

data.title: Should You "Buy" or "Sell" Nvidia Stock Ahead of GTC AI Conference
data.description:
Investors are weighing whether to buy Nvidia ahead of the company's annual GTC AI conference on March 16.

The chipmaker has shifted from a gaming focus to powering artificial intelligence, with AI-related products accounting for about 91% of revenue in the latest fiscal year and helping deliver record sales near $215 billion. GPUs, the specialized chips that handle the heavy computations for training and running AI models, remain central to the business.

Nvidia's early move into AI and continued product development have helped it keep a lead over rivals. CEO Jensen Huang is set to deliver the GTC keynote, an event that has previously included product roadmaps and new launches that move markets. At roughly 21 times forward earnings estimates, some investors may view the stock as richly valued while others see room for further growth.

Shares edged lower about 1% on March 12 amid broader market weakness, but traders will watch GTC closely.

If Huang's keynote delivers meaningful hardware or software updates and positive guidance, the stock could rise after GTC, depending on how investors interpret the announcements.

Analysts' Take

Based on the one year price targets offered by 57 analysts, the average target price for NVIDIA Corp is $262.15 with a high estimate of $432.78 and a low estimate of $138.00. The average target implies a upside of +42.91% from the current price of $183.43.

Based on the consensus recommendation from 65 brokerage firms, NVIDIA Corp's average brokerage recommendation is currently 1.7, indicating a "Outperform" status. The rating scale ranges from 1 to 5, where 1 signifies Strong Buy, and 5 denotes Sell.

data.title: CFTC Issues New Guidance on Sports Prediction Markets — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Nick Devor

The Commodity Futures Trading Commission is in active discussion with sports leagues over sports prediction markets and is advising prediction-market platforms to consider whether markets based on individual player performance, so-called prop bets, are susceptible to manipulation.

The regulator's division of market oversight on Thursday sent an advisory document to "designated contract markets," or DCMs, the CFTC's name for entities licensed to sell event contracts, which are the financial instruments that power prediction markets. The advisory letter marks the regulator's first comprehensive guidance to prediction markets on sports-related trades.

The CFTC on Thursday also issued an advance notice of new rules for prediction markets and requested public comment for those rules.

"In light of the rapid rise in popularity of prediction markets, the division seeks to encourage growth and innovation in these markets while reminding designated contract markets of their regulatory obligations pursuant to the Commodity Exchange Act and Commission regulations," the CFTC said in a press release regarding the guidance.

A primary point of the guidance is to ensure DCMs are in compliance with one of the CFTC's "core principles" for those markets: that DCMs shall list "only contracts that are not readily susceptible to manipulation."

The guidance suggests that prediction markets may want to think twice before listing sports contracts that "resolve or settle based on the action of a single individual." Many prediction markets allow users to bet on an individual player's in-game performance, such as how many points they will score. Those kinds of prop bets are big moneymakers for traditional sportsbooks.

According to the guidance document, the CFTC is in active discussions with sports leagues and their governing bodies "and foresees that appropriate information sharing by these entities with the CFTC may lead to enhanced CFTC oversight capabilities." The guidance encourages prediction markets to work with leagues to ensure that contracts built around sporting events resolve correctly.

Data-sharing and cooperation on integrity monitoring is common in partnerships between sports leagues and traditional sportsbooks like the one operated by DraftKings. However, most of the largest professional sports leagues do not yet have partnerships with any major prediction markets. The National Hockey League, whose partners include Kalshi and Polymarket, is an exception.

The CFTC's acceptance of sports prediction markets has been controversial. More than 20 lawsuits are underway around the country as courts grapple with whether event contracts that pay out when a team wins should be considered sports gambling subject to state regulation. Better Markets, a Washington, D.C., nonprofit that has been critical of the CFTC under the Trump administration, says the regulator's foray into sports distracts from its core mission.

"The CFTC claims that sports betting falls within its remit because it involves a payment that depends on a future occurrence," Benjamin Schiffrin, director of securities policy for Better Markets, said in a press release Thursday. "The CFTC does not — and cannot with a straight face — explain the financial, economic, or commercial consequence of, for example, a particular basketball player scoring or not scoring 20 points in a game on any given night."

Kalshi, the leading prediction market in the U.S., did not immediately respond to Barron's questions regarding its contracts on individual players. International prediction market Polymarket also did not immediately respond. Polymarket has a data partnership with Dow Jones, the publisher of Barron's.

The second document released by the CFTC Thursday was an advance notice of new rules for prediction markets and a request for public comment on those rules. Jake Preiserowicz, a partner at McDermott Will & Schulte and former special counsel at the CFTC, says that when the regulator determines that new rules for its products may have a particular level of public interest, it requests public comment ahead of the release of the rules. It also requests feedback on the rules before implementing them.

This initial request is formatted as a long list of questions, which Preiserowicz says reveal the sorts of rules that the CFTC is considering for prediction markets. Among them: whether to permit prediction market trading on margin for retail customers; which military actions constitute "war" and what related contracts should be allowed; and whether to require DCMs to implement responsible gaming measures like self-exclusion and advertising limits.

Preiserowicz highlights that the CFTC is also considering what degree of insider information should be allowed on prediction markets. The document asks: "Is there some public interest utility if people with an asymmetric information advantage on a particular event contract are able to trade on prediction markets?"

Some in the industry argue that a degree of insider information is necessary for prediction markets to be accurate, while others say it erodes trust. "They want to hear both sides of it," Preiserowicz says of the CFTC.

Kalshi has taken a hard line against insider trading, while Barron's has previously reported that Polymarket social media accounts have promoted instances of potential insider trading occurring on Polymarket.

Preiserowicz notes that throughout the request for public comment document, the CFTC considers the pros and cons for "institutional versus retail, and they ask specific questions about whether we should differentiate." Prediction markets have seen the most adoption among retail investors, while large financial institutions have been slower to jump in.

"For these to really take off and truly be used by the markets, we need institutional," he says.

Write to Nick Devor at nicholas.devor@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: American Electric Power Is Maintained at Neutral by JP Morgan
data.description: This news item displays a headline only and has no other text.

data.title: Europe bulletin: stocks fall, UK tech rules tighten, Tesla enters power
data.description:
European markets faced a turbulent day as geopolitical tensions and regulatory crackdowns rattled investor sentiment.

Stocks slipped across the continent while oil prices surged amid concerns over the Iran conflict and supply disruptions.

At the same time, governments and regulators tightened scrutiny on major technology firms and global corporations, from child safety rules targeting social media giants to tax probes and new trade investigations.

Meanwhile, Tesla’s expansion into Britain’s power market signals fresh competition in the energy sector.

European stocks tumble

European stocks closed lower on Thursday, with the pan-European Stoxx 600 ending about 0.7% down as investors weighed the Iran conflict.

Markets stayed focused on energy after the International Energy Agency said it would release 400 million barrels of oil to ease supply disruptions linked to the Iran war, though it gave no fixed timeline.

Despite that move, oil prices jumped more than 8% overnight, with Brent crude touching $100 a barrel.

Investors also digested the Trump administration’s announcement of new trade investigations into the European Union and more than a dozen other countries.

​UK regulators slam tech giants over child safety

Britain’s media and privacy regulators have ordered major social media platforms to step up efforts to keep children off their services.

The regulators warned that the tech giants are failing to enforce basic age limits.

Under the latest phase of the Online Safety Act, Ofcom told Meta’s Facebook and Instagram, Roblox, Snapchat, TikTok, and YouTube to spell out by April 30 how they will toughen age checks.

The regulators want the social media companies to curb contact from strangers and stop testing new products on minors.

The Information Commissioner’s Office separately urged companies to deploy “modern, viable” age-verification tools to block under-13s.

Tesla to supply electricity in UK

Tesla has won regulatory clearance to enter Britain’s retail power market, expanding its push beyond electric cars and batteries.

UK energy watchdog Ofgem granted an electricity supply licence to Tesla Energy Ventures, allowing the unit to sell power to households and businesses across Great Britain.

The license is limited to electricity supply and sits alongside a separate generation licence held by Tesla Motors since 2020.

The move brings a high-profile new competitor into an already crowded market dominated by British Gas, EDF, Octopus Energy, and others.

Italian prosecutors seek Amazon's trial

Milan prosecutors want Amazon's European unit and four executives to stand trial on charges of dodging around 1.2 billion euros ($1.38 billion) in Italian taxes, a Reuters report said on Thursday.

The case targets alleged VAT evasion on online sales from 2019 to 2021, where Amazon's algorithm let non-EU sellers avoid declaring their identities and paying the tax.

Prosecutors took the unusual step despite Amazon's December settlement paying 527 million euros plus interest to Italy's tax agency.

Amazon insists it follows all tax rules and plans a strong defense. The outcome could shake up its model across Europe.
